title: lithocarpus echinulatus
intro: Lithocarpus echinulatus, also known as the Chiltepin Oak, is a deciduous tree species native to the tropical and subtropical regions of Central and South America. This plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly in folk medicine to treat various health issues. The Chiltepin Oak is valued for its health benefits, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, as well as its potential to lower cholesterol levels and improve cardiovascular health. Lithocarpus echinulatus can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are often used to alleviate symptoms of fever, cough, and respiratory issues.
